steps.title: Identify what proves the professor can't adjust
steps.content: We need the professor's own words expressing discomfort with the country estate—not external judgment of him, not his general background, but his direct reaction to the setting.

steps.title: Match the quotation to the claim
steps.content:
Choice C — Professor Serebrakoff says to Helena, "I am used to my library and the lecture hall and to the esteem and admiration of my colleagues. Now I suddenly find myself plunged in this wilderness [the estate], condemned to see the same stupid people from morning till night and listen to their futile conversation." directly shows the professor contrasting his familiar library and lecture hall life with the estate as a “wilderness” of “stupid people,” articulating his fundamental incompatibility with country-estate life.

The alternatives focus on Vanya's changed opinion of the professor, Serebrakoff's self-description, or Serebrakoff's fear of Vanya—none address his struggle with the country estate itself.

The estate-discomfort quotation is correct.
